title: Workspace-hygiene S2: clean-exit marker finalize - new core.ClearCompletedCycleMarker (NOT SealCycle: seal is abandon-semantics and would poison faillearn with OperatorReset lessons on every healthy boundary); loop's terminal exit clears a COMPLETED final cycle's cycle-state.json so the next launch needs no reset
summary: Slice S2 of docs/plans/workspace-hygiene-2026-07.md (READ FIRST). Every max_cycles exit leaves the final cycle's marker active with a dead owner. DEPENDS ON s1 (runlease.OwnerLive) - defer if unshipped.
root_cause: The loop exit path never touches cycle-state; the only marker-clearing primitive (SealCycle) archives the run dir to .reset-<ts> AND writes failure-learning records - wrong semantics for a cleanly completed cycle.
fix: Per plan S2: new core/cycle_finalize.go ClearCompletedCycleMarker(evolveDir, FinalizeOptions{Now, LeaseTTL, PidAlive}) (bool, error): resolve via ResolveCycleStatePath (supervisor sees global file; fleet lanes' per-run markers untouched); guards cycle_id==0 / cycle_id>lastCycleNumber (protects --resume, quota-pause, signal exits) / runlease.OwnerLive => no-op; else os.Remove. Call WARN-only at cmd_loop.go terminal-exit sites (before lr.emit ~766-779 + resumed_complete ~286); NOT on signal/quota-pause paths. RED FIRST: TestClearCompletedCycleMarker_RemovesTerminalMarker (asserts marker gone, state.json byte-untouched, NO .reset-* dir, NO lesson written - pins the not-SealCycle semantics), _PreservesUnfinishedMarker, _LiveOwnerUntouched, TestLoop_MaxCyclesExit_ClearsCompletedMarker + TestLoop_SignalExit_PreservesMarker (loopOrchOverride/wireOrchestratorDepsFn seams).
acceptance: max_cycles exit with completed final cycle leaves cycle-state EMPTY (next evolve loop needs no reset); FAIL final cycle preserves marker (resumable evidence).
No .reset-* archive, no faillearn writes on the clean path (regression-tested).
go vet, -race, gofmt -s, apicover-named for new core exports green.
notes: Weight 0.90. This is the slice that ends the operator-seal-every-boundary routine.
